nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: Nightly regression run at 02:00 server time. Adjust cron as needed.

nodes.name: CI/CD Webhook Trigger
nodes.notes: On-demand trigger from CI/CD after a deploy. Body: { tags: string[], build_id: string }. Protected with a Header Auth credential (shared secret) so only CI/CD can invoke it.

nodes.name: Normalize Trigger Input
nodes.notes: Unifies the Schedule Trigger and Webhook Trigger into one common shape: { source, tags, build_id }.

nodes.name: Initialize Run
nodes.notes: Generates a unique run_id and timestamp carried through the whole run. Also initialises per-run accumulator arrays in workflow static data (keyed by run_id) so aggregation works and concurrent runs never mix results.

nodes.name: Load Test Suite
nodes.notes: Reads all rows from the 'Test Suite' tab. Column schema documented in README / sample_test_suite.csv.

nodes.name: Validate & Filter Tests
nodes.notes: Filters to active + tag-matching tests, coerces JSON-in-cells (headers/body/expected_key_values/expected_schema/dynamic_fields) into real objects, normalizes types. Invalid rows are skipped (not fatal) and recorded per run for the final report.

nodes.name: Batch Controller
nodes.notes: Controls execution concurrency (default 5). Output 0 = done -> Aggregate. Output 1 = loop -> per-test execution chain, which feeds back into this node's input to continue the loop.

nodes.name: Route by Test Type
nodes.notes: Routes each test to its execution method. Unsupported test_type values fall through to 'extra' -> Normalize Result Shape marks them unsupported instead of erroring.

nodes.name: Execute API Test
nodes.notes: Executes the API test as configured in the sheet. neverError=true so 4xx/5xx responses are captured as data (that's the point of a regression test), not thrown as node errors.

nodes.name: Execute DB Query
nodes.notes: Runs the configured read-only verification query for db-type tests.

nodes.name: Trigger UI Runner
nodes.notes: Delegates browser automation to an external Playwright/Selenium microservice; n8n only orchestrates. Expected response: { status, screenshot_match_score, extracted_text }.

nodes.name: Normalize Result Shape
nodes.notes: Maps the three raw execution shapes (API/DB/UI) into one common { expected_*, actual } contract. Re-reads test config from Batch Controller so expected values survive the execution nodes. Adjust field mapping to your real API/runner response shapes.

nodes.name: Deterministic Assertion Engine
nodes.notes: Zero-cost first pass: status code, schema-required fields, and exact key/value match on everything NOT in dynamic_fields. Pure semantic assertions (*_semantic keys) always route to AI. Unsupported test types short-circuit to UNSUPPORTED.

nodes.name: Needs AI Review?
nodes.notes: TRUE -> escalate to Claude for semantic adjudication (only AMBIGUOUS results). FALSE -> deterministic PASS/UNSUPPORTED is final, no AI call (cost control).

nodes.name: Claude - Semantic Diff
nodes.notes: Only reached for ambiguous deterministic results. temperature=0 for reproducible verdicts. Requires an 'Anthropic API Key' HTTP Header Auth credential with header name 'x-api-key'. Update model per docs.anthropic.com if needed.

nodes.name: Parse AI Verdict
nodes.notes: Defensively parses Claude's JSON (strips code fences). Any parse/timeout failure degrades gracefully to NEEDS_REVIEW rather than crashing the run.

nodes.name: Merge Final Verdict
nodes.notes: Produces the final per-test record (deterministic-only or AI-adjudicated) AND pushes it into per-run static data so aggregation has the full result set. Feeds back into Batch Controller to continue the loop.

nodes.name: Aggregate Run Results
nodes.notes: Runs once on Batch Controller 'done'. Reads the accumulated per-run results from static data, computes counts, clusters failures by (endpoint, root_cause_category), collects stale-expectation suggestions, then clears the per-run accumulators.

nodes.name: Claude - Executive Summary
nodes.notes: Runs once per run (flat cost regardless of suite size). Consumes only aggregated counts/failures, not raw payloads.

nodes.name: Parse Summary JSON
nodes.notes: Parses the executive summary; degrades gracefully to a MEDIUM-risk placeholder rather than blocking reporting if the AI call fails.

nodes.name: Risk Threshold Exceeded?
nodes.notes: TRUE -> page the team + file tickets. FALSE -> routine, non-paging summary only.

nodes.name: Slack - Critical Alert
nodes.notes: Pages the QA-critical channel with @here. Requires a Slack Bot credential with chat:write scope. Bot must be invited to #qa-critical-alerts.

nodes.name: Split Failure Clusters
nodes.notes: One ticket per FAILURE CLUSTER (grouped by endpoint + root_cause_category), not one per test - prevents ticket storms during a systemic outage. Emits zero items when there are no FAIL clusters, which correctly skips ticket creation.

nodes.name: Create Jira Tickets
nodes.notes: One ticket per failure cluster. Requires a Jira Basic Auth credential (email + API token). Replace REPLACE_JIRA_DOMAIN and the project key 'QA'. Uses Jira Cloud ADF description format (API v3).

nodes.name: Slack - Routine Summary
nodes.notes: Non-paging routine summary posted after every non-critical run. Bot must be invited to #qa-regression.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO qa_run_history (run_id, started_at, source, build_id, pass_count, fail_count, flaky_count, needs_review_count, risk_rating, headline, narrative)
VALUES ($1, now(), $2, $3, $4, $5, $6, $7, $8, $9, $10);

options.queryReplacement: ={{ $('Parse Summary JSON').first().json.run_id }},={{ $('Parse Summary JSON').first().json.source }},={{ $('Parse Summary JSON').first().json.build_id }},={{ $('Parse Summary JSON').first().json.counts.pass }},={{ $('Parse Summary JSON').first().json.counts.fail }},={{ $('Parse Summary JSON').first().json.counts.flaky }},={{ $('Parse Summary JSON').first().json.counts.needs_review }},={{ $('Parse Summary JSON').first().json.risk_rating }},={{ $('Parse Summary JSON').first().json.headline }},={{ $('Parse Summary JSON').first().json.narrative }}

nodes.name: Store Run History
nodes.notes: Persists run-level history via PARAMETERIZED query ($1..$10) - no SQL injection, no manual quote-escaping. Requires a qa_run_history table (see README for DDL). Runs regardless of which reporting branch fired.

nodes.name: Triggered By Webhook?
nodes.notes: Only a webhook-triggered run has a waiting HTTP connection to respond to.

nodes.name: Respond to Webhook
nodes.notes: Lets a CI/CD pipeline gate a deploy on this run's result.
